summary: A broad automatic permuter pass over high-percent unmatched functions touched 35 files across fighter, item, interface, game-mode, stage, effect, library, menu, and toy code. The bot report says the PR produced +6896 matched-code bytes, +20 matched-data bytes, 50 new matches, 2 unmatched-item improvements, and 1 unmatched-item regression in ftKb_Init .sdata. The diff evidence shows many small source-shape edits typical of permuter output, including temporary variables named new_var, helper inlines, expression splitting, and removal of a large block from ftKb_Init.

what_changed: The author described the work as running the permuter on all high-percent functions for about one minute.
35 files changed with 202 additions and 8100 deletions.
The bot report recorded +0.18% matched code, +6896 matched-code bytes, +20 matched-data bytes, and 50 new matches.
The largest deletion was in src/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftKb_Init.c, where changed-file metadata reports 7962 deleted lines.

The diff excerpt shows source-shape changes such as introducing new_var/new_var2 temporaries, extracting a pointer argument into a temporary before a call, adding a tiny inline wrapper returning &Effect_AllocData, and splitting a ternary expression into a local variable.
The bot report also recorded 2 improvements in unmatched items and 1 unmatched-item regression: ftKb_Init .sdata moved from 19.55% to 0.00%.

smart_moves.text: The PR used a broad, short permuter sweep to harvest many small high-percent matches quickly, then relied on the automated report to enumerate exact matched symbols and regressions.
The report makes text/data tradeoffs visible: many new text matches landed while ftKb_Init .sdata regressed.

decomp_lessons.lesson: Automatic permuter output can close many near-matching functions, but PR evidence should be reviewed symbol-by-symbol because the same sweep may introduce mechanical source shapes and data-section regressions.
For broad permuter PRs, treat the bot report as a ledger: record new 100% text matches separately from unmatched data improvements and regressions.

naming_conventions.name: new_var / new_var2
naming_conventions.convention: These names appear as permuter-introduced temporaries in the diff; the PR evidence does not support treating them as a desired naming convention.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Introduce temporaries to affect register allocation or expression ordering.
examples: Effect_RemoveUserData assigns effect through new_var/new_var2 before field access and HSD_ObjFree.
ftCo_80097F38 stores the ternary motion-state result in int new_var before Fighter_ChangeMotionState.
ftKb_SpecialN_800F15D8 stores ft_80459B88.hats[FTKIND_PICHU] in a KirbyHatStruct* temporary before the call.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use a tiny inline wrapper to alter a call argument shape.
examples: Effect_RemoveUserData_inline returns &Effect_AllocData and is passed to HSD_ObjFree instead of passing &Effect_AllocData directly.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use automated report output to identify exact new 100% matches and regressions.
examples: Bot report lists 50 new matches and a ftKb_Init .sdata regression.

notes: Changed-file metadata and bot report are strong for file list, counts, matched symbols, and reported regression.
The raw diff is truncated, so file-specific source-shape observations are limited to visible excerpts and the changed-file list.
There were no review comments and no human reviewer feedback in the loaded slice.
The bot report provides match/regression metrics, but the intake slice does not include local build commands, objdiff screenshots, or human validation notes.
The PR was merged according to metadata, but no acceptance rationale is available beyond merge state and bot report.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Broad permuter sweeps need a report ledger
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2200 shows a short automatic permuter pass can produce many small 100% matches, but the resulting postmortem should record matched-code wins, matched-data changes, and regressions separately rather than treating the sweep as uniformly beneficial.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Permuter artifacts are evidence of matching tactics, not naming style
accepted_candidate_records.text: The diff shows temporaries such as new_var/new_var2 and tiny inline wrappers used in matched functions. These are useful search terms for recognizing permuter-driven source-shape edits, but the PR does not establish them as preferred source style.

source_update_candidates.title: Record automatic permuter reports as match/regression ledgers
source_update_candidates.text: For broad automatic permuter PRs, require the postmortem or review note to separate new 100% text matches from data-section changes and regressions, because PR #2200 shows both many new text matches and a ftKb_Init .sdata regression in the same sweep.
source_update_candidates.reason: This is consistent with existing verification/data-risk standards, but curator should decide whether PR #2200 adds enough historical support for an explicit permuter-specific note.

rejection_notes: Do not promote new_var/new_var2 as accepted naming convention; the evidence only shows these names were used by or from the permuter output.
Do not infer reviewer approval of source style from merge state; no review comments are present.
Do not claim all 35 touched functions were manually validated; the loaded evidence only includes the bot report and PR body.
Do not treat the ftKb_Init .sdata regression as acceptable policy; the evidence only reports that it occurred.
